title: Market Brief — Friday, April 10, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce across the board. H100 availability sits at 20% (76/301), H200 at a severe 12% (65/341), and B200 is the loosest at 28% (128/405) as Blackwell supply ramps. H200 spot jumped 17.5% overnight to $3.03/hr on tight supply, while H100 dropped 7.5% as workloads migrate to newer silicon and utilization fell 8.6pp over 30 days.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce across the board. H100 availability sits at 20% (76/301), H200 at a severe 12% (65/341), and B200 is the loosest at 28% (128/405) as Blackwell supply ramps. H200 spot jumped 17.5% overnight to $3.03/hr on tight supply, while H100 dropped 7.5% as workloads migrate to newer silicon and utilization fell 8.6pp over 30 days.

B200 at TCO-adjusted cost of $3.49/M tokens is the only GPU where medium-tier inference turns a profit. H200 operators selling Llama 70B at medium tier face a loss of $2.74 per million tokens against a $7.26 TCO-adjusted cost. Provider grades: OpenAI Neutral, Anthropic Neutral, Google Strong, DeepSeek Burning Cash at $0.38/M output on R1 versus $2.50 list price, and open-source Burning Cash at scale.

B200 is undervalued: 96.6% annualized price volatility signals a market still discovering fair value, yet it already delivers the best inference margin stack. H100 is overvalued for new deployments given falling utilization and the H200/B200 cost crossover — sell H100 exposure, go long B200 capacity contracts before availability tightens below 20%.
bullets: 12% H200 availability signals a severe shortage with only 65 of 341 GPUs free.
28% B200 availability marks the only GPU class above the scarcity threshold.
$3.03 H200 spot median jumped 17.5% day-over-day, the largest single move in the stack.
21.41 LLMflation index means API pricing has fallen roughly 79% since GPT-4 launch.
$0.38 DeepSeek V3.2 output pricing undercuts every Western frontier model by 5x or more.
87.7% H200 utilization surged 21.7 percentage points in 30 days, confirming demand concentration on next-gen silicon.
